data.body:
[G3 child-coverage audit — parent 72d5 (Migrate the LLM gates onto the workflow engine) vs child B5 (f12d: Cut the claim/close gates over to the workflow path, signing intact)] Clean — no findings.

4-bucket audit of the parent's acceptance/success criteria against THIS child, with whole-roster absence cross-check:

- Parent AC4 (the bespoke orchestrator path is cut over to the workflow path BEHIND THE GATE(S) once parity holds; the close/claim gates keep working — signed verdicts/attestations intact): FULLY covered by THIS child. B5 wires _commands/claim.py (plan-review precheck) + _commands/transition.py (close verify) + the review_plan/verify_completion entrypoints to dispatch through the engine workflow behind the verify.gate_engine config flag (default workflow, bespoke fallback). Its AC2 preserves byte-compatible signed verdicts/attestations (material binding + manifest prefixes unchanged → previously signed tickets still verify, CI signature checks pass), faithfully mirroring AC4's "close/claim gates keep working — signed verdicts/attestations intact." Its AC3 supplies integration tests for claim+close pass/fail through the workflow path with intact signing, and keeps the bespoke path reachable behind the flag as a fallback — the "behind the gate(s)" clause. This is B5's primary deliverable and it maps 1:1 onto AC4; no over-reach.

- Parent AC1 (plan-review as engine workflow + production batch-runner extracted from sizing.py): structural / not-this-child — FULLY covered by B2 (6b06) + B1 (723c). B5 consumes those (dispatches verdict production through gates/plan-review.yaml via ProductionBatchRunner) but does not own the construction; no regression introduced.

- Parent AC2 (verify_completion as engine workflow, existing step kinds): structural / not-this-child — FULLY covered by B3 (dc58). B5 consumes the completion-verification.yaml workflow at the close gate; the B5 cutover note (terminal-arm completion_verdict surfacing) is an engine fix it lands to make the consumption work, not a duplicate of B3's migration.

- Parent AC3 (planned-trace parity harness, offline FakeRunner corpus, CI): structural / not-this-child — FULLY covered by B4 (101a). B5 depends on B4 ("parity must hold first") and upgrades the harness's verify call-mode assertion to cross-assert workflow==bespoke once it makes verify dynamic — a refinement of B4's deliverable, consistent, not a coverage gap.

- Parent AC5 (remove throwaway sample stubs; preserve shims; retain epic-A demo): structural / not-this-child — FULLY covered by B6 (7e48).

- Parent AC6 / success criterion (exactly two real LLM workflows + the one retained sample; only real workflows remain; make lint + full suite pass): primarily B6 (7e48); B5 contributes its own "make lint + full test suite pass" AC, reinforcing the green-suite portion.

- Parent success criterion ("plan-review and completion-verification run on the ONE workflow-engine substrate ... validated by offline planned-trace parity against the bespoke implementations; only real workflows remain"): the cross-roster union (B1/B2/B3/B4 + this child B5 + 0408 + B-DM 5506) covers substrate/prompt-library/parity/cutover; B5 specifically delivers the "run on the ONE substrate at the live gate" half by flipping the gates onto the workflow path while preserving signing.

Whole-roster absence cross-check: every parent acceptance/success criterion is covered by at least one child in the complete sibling roster; B5 fully owns AC4 (its responsibility) and leaves no parent criterion uncovered, nor introduces a new uncovered one. Ordering is consistent (B5 depends on B4 parity; B5 blocks B-RETIRE 961b which removes the fallback flag and the bespoke path — deps present: f12d blocks 961b and 7e48). No G3 finding.
